# t66 — --no-run on a mutation: forces --who/--why (exit 2) and, when signed,
# skips the pipeline and writes an audit skip-record instead.
exec mtt init
cp events.yaml .mtt/config.yaml

# bare bypass → aggregated missing who/why, nothing persisted
! exec mtt add 'x' --no-run
stderr 'who, why'
exec mtt list --ids
! stdout 't1'

# signed bypass → task created, pipeline skipped, audit record written
exec mtt add 'x' --no-run --who me --why testing
stdout 'created t1'
! exists created-t1-create
exists .mtt/audit.log
grep '"action":"add --no-run"' .mtt/audit.log
grep '"who":"me"' .mtt/audit.log

# rm --force --no-run → ONE record with the full invocation shape (pin iii)
exec mtt rm t1 --force --no-run --who me --why cleanup
grep '"action":"rm --force --no-run"' .mtt/audit.log
! exists deleted-t1-delete

-- events.yaml --
version: 1
project: {name: evtest}
events:
  task:
    create:
      post: ['touch created-{{.ID}}-{{.Event}}']
    delete:
      post: ['touch deleted-{{.ID}}-{{.Event}}']
types:
  - name: task
    prefix: t
    default: true
    statuses:
      - {name: tbd, kind: initial}
      - {name: doing, kind: active}
      - {name: done, kind: terminal}
    transitions:
      - {from: tbd, to: doing}
      - {from: doing, to: done}
